calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-29-2014 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-27-2014 Set For Case Management Conference On Jan-14-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Nexant Inc., Deborah Rimmler, An Individual, Christine Harper, Karen Neuendorff Motion For Summary Judgment And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment (For Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Oct-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Nexant Inc., Deborah Rimmler, An Individual, Christine Harper, Karen Neuendorff Motion For Summary Judgment (For Tentative Ruling Only) is a duplicate entry. (302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Susan Pangborn, Esq. of Kilpatrick Townsend & Stockton LLP, T:415.273.4763 for defendant and moving party;
Joshua Edmondson, Esq. of the Law Office of John R. Settlich, T:909.481.6400, for plaintiff and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: A R G U E D. After arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS NEXANT INC., DEBORAH RIMMLER, CHRISTINE HARPER and KAREN NEUENDORFF's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendants met their initial burden showing there are no triable issues of material fact. The only evidence submitted in opposition is Plaintiff's declaration. The declaration is largely conclusory and speculative. The declaration does not give rise to any triable issues of material fact. As to the discrimination and wrongful termination claims, Defendant met their burden of showing that there were legitimate business reasons for Plaintiff's termination - Plaintiff's unsatisfactory performance regarding her interactions with co-workers and Plaintiff's medical condition making her unable to work for an indefinite period of time. (Defendants' Issue No. 3, Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 12-17; Issue No. 10, UMF 1-7.) Plaintiff has not met her burden of producing "substantial, responsive evidence that the employer's showing was untrue or pretextual." (Hersant v. Cal. Dept. of Social Services (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 997, 1004-05.) As to the harassment and hostile work environment claims, Defendants met their initial burden showing that the alleged acts of harassment were not severe or pervasive. (Issue No. 9, UMF 1-14.) Plaintiff has not met her burden of raising a triable issue of material fact, as her declaration only states specific facts about a few incidences. Acts of harassment cannot be "occasional, isolated, sporadic, or trivial." (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1043.) The complaint alleges that Plaintiff was retaliated against pursuant to Cal. Lab. Code secs. 1102.5 and 2856. (Complaint, par. 95.) Defendants met their burden showing Plaintiff cannot establish the material elements of the claim. (Issue 13, UMF 1-5.) Plaintiff's declaration states no facts to the contrary. As to the breach of contract and breach of covenant of good faith causes of action, Defendants met their initial burden establishing that Plaintiff cannot set forth facts to establish this cause of action. (Issue 8, UMF 1-14.) Plaintiff's declaration does not set forth facts to the contrary, specifically because Plaintiff's declaration does not state any facts regarding the existence of a contract. Defendants met their initial burden on the cause of action for negligent retention and supervision. (Issue 6, UMF 1-8.) Plaintiff's declaration sets forth no facts that the employer knew or should have known that an employee presented an undue harm to third persons. (Federico v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 1207, 1214.) As to the promissory fraud and negligent misrepresentation claims, Defendants met their initial burden. (Issue 14, UMF 1-6; Issue 16, UMF 1-7.) Plaintiff's declaration states no facts that establish that Defendants did not perform as promised. Defendants met their initial burden on the IIED claim. (Issue 15, 1-8.) Plaintiff's declaration do not state facts sufficient to give rise to a triable issue that Defendants' engaged in conduct "so extreme as to exceed all bounds of that usually tolerated in a civilized community." (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1050.) Lastly, the negligence claim fails as a matter of law because it is barred by the exclusivity provision of the Worker's Compensation Act. (Fermino v. Fedco (1994) 7 Cal.4th 701, 714-15.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR# 11185.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2014 Set For Trial On Nov-10-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar Jan-08-2014 Text Ruling
